# Proverbs 28

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-INDEX (verse table + nav; auto-generated — safe to regenerate) -->

## Verses (28)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | The evil man goes running away when no man is after him, but the upright are without fear, like the lion. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Because of the sin of the land, its troubles are increased; but by a man of wisdom and knowledge they will be put out like a fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | A man of wealth who is cruel to the poor is like a violent rain causing destruction of food. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Those who have no respect for the law give praise to the evil-doer; but such as keep the law are against him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Evil men have no knowledge of what is right; but those who go after the Lord have knowledge of all things. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | Better is the poor man whose ways are upright, than the man of wealth whose ways are not straight. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | He who keeps the law is a wise son, but he who keeps company with feasters puts shame on his father. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | He who makes his wealth greater by taking interest, only gets it together for him who has pity on the poor. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | As for the man whose ear is turned away from hearing the law, even his prayer is disgusting. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Anyone causing the upright to go wandering in an evil way, will himself go down into the hole he has made; but the upright will have good things for their heritage. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | The man of wealth seems to himself to be wise, but the poor man who has sense has a low opinion of him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | When the upright do well, there is great glory; but when evil-doers are lifted up, men do not let themselves be seen. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | He who keeps his sins secret will not do well; but one who is open about them, and gives them up, will get mercy. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | Happy is the man in whom is the fear of the Lord at all times; but he whose heart is hard will come into trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Like a loud-voiced lion and a wandering bear, is an evil ruler over a poor people. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | The prince who has no sense is a cruel ruler; but he who has no desire to get profit for himself will have long life. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | One who has been the cause of a man's death will go in flight to the underworld: let no man give him help. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | He whose ways are upright will be safe, but sudden will be the fall of him whose ways are twisted. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | By ploughing his land a man will have bread in full measure; but he who goes after good-for-nothing persons will be poor enough. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | A man of good faith will have great blessing, but one attempting to get wealth quickly will not go free from punishment. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | It is not good to have respect for a man's position: for a man will do wrong for a bit of bread. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | He who is ever desiring wealth goes running after money, and does not see that need will come on him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | He who says words of protest to a man will later have more approval than one who says smooth words with his tongue. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | He who takes from his father or his mother what is theirs by right, and says, It is no sin; is the same as a taker of life. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | He who is ever desiring profit is a cause of fighting; but he who puts his faith in the Lord will be made fat. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | He whose faith is in himself is foolish; but everyone walking wisely will be kept safe. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | He who gives to the poor will never be in need, but great curses will be on him who gives no attention to them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | When evil-doers are lifted up, men take cover; but when destruction overtakes them, the upright are increased. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |

<!-- END AUTO-INDEX -->
